rationale.R1.2: The section makes sweeping universal claims ('all instruction types', 'full spectrum of modern LLMs') that must cohere with the narrow empirical base (600 prompts, 4 models, 3 categories). Logical consistency between premises and conclusions is central if substantive critique is requested.
rationale.R1.3: Pearson r=0.71 on 4 open-weight 7-9B models is presented as evidence of generalizability 'across the full range of modern LLMs'; whether this evidence actually licenses those claims is load-bearing if an evidence-alignment review is what is wanted.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal a substantive critique of whether the empirical evidence (4 models, 600 prompts) actually supports the universal claims made (e.g., 'all instruction types', 'full spectrum of modern LLMs'), or do you want sentence-level writing polish and tightening of the prose?
